Title: Harnessing GhGRDP1 natural variation for enhanced cotton seed yield: a rhamnose-dependent strategy in breeding

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cell wall assembly and seed development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in stem, leaf, stamen, silique, pistil, and seeds; highly expressed in developing seeds
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in stem, leaf, stamen, silique, pistil, and seeds; highly expressed in developing seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of seed size and weight in Arabidopsis; knockout reduces seed size
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; promoter-GUS expression analysis; seed morphometry; heterologous GhGRDP1 overexpression comparison
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtGRDP1 promoter for GUS reporter; CaMV 35S for GhGRDP1 ectopic expression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports conserved use of GRDP1 homologs to improve seed size in dicots
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to demonstrate functional conservation of GRDP1 in seed-size control across angiosperms
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGRDP1 is highly expressed in developing seeds and multiple tissues. 2. Atgrdp1 mutants have shorter, narrower, and lighter seeds. 3. These data support a conserved role of GRDP1 homologs in seed-size regulation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of grain size and 1000-grain weight in rice; knockout reduces grain size
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; grain morphometry; selective sweep analysis using resequencing data
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Rice resequencing dataset of 718 accessions from National Genomics Data Center; accession not specified in text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate domestication and breeding gene for improving grain size in monocots
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in a selective sweep region between O. sativa and O. rufipogon, suggesting domestication-associated selection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGRDP1 knockout significantly decreases grain length, grain width, and 1000-grain weight. 2. OsGRDP1 appears to be under selection during rice domestication. 3. These data support conserved GRDP1 function in seed/grain size control across monocots and eudicots.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall precursor affecting seed growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal seed size in Arabidopsis; knockout reduces seed weight, length, and width
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports the broader importance of UDP-Rha synthesis for seed-size engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as cross-species functional support that UDP-Rha content influences seed size
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Atrhm1 mutants show reduced seed weight, length, and width. 2. This supports the hypothesis that UDP-L-rhamnose availability contributes to seed size determination.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for primary cell wall polysaccharide synthesis and cell wall assembly; supports ovule/cell expansion and seed development
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Trifunctional RHM enzyme with N-terminal UDP-Glc 4,6-dehydratase activity and C-terminal UDP-4K6DG 3,5-epimerase/UDP-4-keto-L-rhamnose 4-keto-reductase activities
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assay conditions with UDP-Glc, NAD+, NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the multi-step conversion of UDP-D-glucose into UDP-L-rhamnose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; activity is enhanced by GhGRDP1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GhRHM1 activity is associated with increased UDP-L-rhamnose production that supports ovule expansion and larger seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction assays with GhGRDP1 (LCI, Co-IP, pull-down, BiFC); in vitro UDP-Rha synthesis/HPLC enzyme activity assay; Arabidopsis Atrhm1 CRISPR mutant seed phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-IP LC-MS/MS interactor screening; targeted metabolomics; no accession reported specifically for GhRHM1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme target for enhancing rhamnose/UDP-Rha production, though the paper proposes its co-activator GhGRDP1 as a more practical breeding/engineering target
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis AtRHM1 knockout reduced seed weight, length and width, supporting a conserved role of UDP-Rha in seed size regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhRHM1 physically interacts with GhGRDP1 in vivo and in vitro. 2. GhRHM1 converts UDP-D-glucose to UDP-L-rhamnose, and its product supports primary cell wall assembly. 3. Addition of GhGRDP1 significantly increased GhRHM1 reaction efficiency and UDP-Rha accumulation, linking this pathway to seed enlargement.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for primary cell wall assembly, promoting ovule cell expansion and seed development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycine-rich domain protein with two N-terminal DUF1399 domains and a C-terminal short glycine-rich domain (sGRD)
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm; colocalizes with endoplasmic reticulum marker
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural haplotype variation with 1-bp deletion causing premature termination codon and nonsense-mediated mRNA decay
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nonsense-mediated mRNA decay triggered by a 1-bp deletion and PTC in GhGRDP1HAP2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances GhRHM1 enzymatic activity, increasing UDP-L-rhamnose production
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-translational activation through physical interaction with enzyme target
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: UDP-L-rhamnose synthesis and downstream primary cell wall assembly/osmolyte-linked ovule expansion
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator and activator of GhRHM1; decisive because natural and transgenic variation consistently alters UDP-Rha-associated seed size and yield
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts directly with GhRHM1; no higher-order named complex reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates cotton seed size, seed weight and seed yield without compromising fiber quality; higher expression promotes larger seeds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/eQTL colocalization; KASP haplotyping; degradome and GUS/qPCR assays for NMD; overexpression, RNAi and CRISPR knockout in cotton; heterologous overexpression/knockout in Arabidopsis and rice; localization; Co-IP, pull-down, BiFC, LCI; enzyme activity assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Negative correlation with lint percent and protein content; high-expression/large-seed state may oppose LP improvement, though no obvious fiber quality penalty was observed
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Population resequencing; degradome SRA SRR12640449; SRR3105380; SRR13503521; SRR11743053; RNA-seq PRJNA1259138; targeted metabolomics; Co-IP LC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in cotton and Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for cotton breeding to improve seed size/yield; GhGRDP1HAP1/HAP2 marker useful for selection; can be combined with fiber- or embryo-specific promoters, prime editing, and stacking with LP-, oil-, or gossypol-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The 1-bp deletion haplotype GhGRDP1HAP2 is unique to G. hirsutum, arose from races, and was strongly selected during domestication; orthologous GRDP1 genes also affect seed size in Arabidopsis and rice; both N-terminal binding region and C-terminal sGRD are required for full activation of GhRHM1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhGRDP1 is the major experimentally studied regulator underlying natural variation in cotton seed index and lint percent. 2. A 1-bp deletion in exon 8 creates a PTC that triggers NMD, lowers GhGRDP1 transcript abundance, and produces smaller seeds with higher lint percent. 3. GhGRDP1 positively regulates seed size and yield in cotton, and its orthologs have conserved seed-size functions in Arabidopsis and rice. 4. GhGRDP1 physically interacts with and activates GhRHM1, increasing UDP-L-rhamnose synthesis linked to cell wall assembly, osmolyte accumulation, ovule cell expansion, and seed enlargement.
